message:
fix(engine): add instruction bounds check and cap Vec pre-allocations to prevent memory bombs

Three targeted safety hardening fixes:

1. Instruction pointer bounds check (baseline_interpreter.rs):
   Replace unchecked `module.instructions[self.ip]` array index with
   `.get(self.ip).ok_or(InstructionOutOfBounds { ip, count })` — returns
   a clean error instead of panicking if the IP escapes the instruction
   stream (possible with malformed bytecode or a jump-target bug).

2. Cap Vec::with_capacity for untrusted lengths (stdlib.rs):
   read_array_elements(), read_map_entries(), read_set_values() all
   pre-allocate based on object length properties that come from user
   JS code. A malicious `array.length = 2^32` would OOM the process.
   Now capped to `.min(1024)` — the loop still runs to completion,
   but the initial allocation is bounded. Items beyond 1024 trigger
   normal Vec growth instead of a single enormous allocation.

3. Same cap for IR3 lowering (lowering_pipeline.rs):
   Construct instruction arg_regs and template-literal part_regs
   Vec::with_capacity calls now use `.min(1024)` to prevent untrusted
   arg_count / quasi_count from causing excessive pre-allocation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
